title: STEAL MY CAROUSEL SYSTEM
description:
The 6 Rules Behind Every Carousel I Post — Plus The Exact AI Prompts I Use To Structure Them.
BUILT BY DHRUV SOLANKI / @DHRUVWORLD
body:
The 6 Rules Behind Every Carousel I Post — Plus The Exact AI Prompts I Use To Structure Them.
BUILT BY DHRUV SOLANKI / @DHRUVWORLD

6 RULES. EVERY CAROUSEL. NO EXCEPTIONS.
Most creators focus on content.
I focus on systems.
Because content disappears.
Systems compound.
This framework helps me create AI content, founder content, business content, and personal brand content without staring at a blank screen every day.
These are the exact rules behind every carousel I publish.

RULE 01
The Cover Has One Job: Stop The Scroll
Your cover is not competing with other carousels.
It's competing against:
• Reels
• Friends
• Ads
• Memes
• News
You have less than one second.
Use it wisely.
THE TRAP
Writing:
"5 AI Tools For Creators"
Too predictable.
Too safe.
Too easy to ignore.
THE FIX
Lead with:
• A promise
• A result
• A strong opinion
• A surprising number
Maximum 8 words.
Examples:
✓ 7 AI Tools I Use Daily
✓ Most Creators Are Using AI Wrong
✓ My Content System Runs Itself
✓ This Replaced My Entire Workflow
THE CHECK
Would you stop scrolling?
If not, rewrite it.

RULE 02
Slide 2 Confirms The Hook
The cover gets the swipe.
Slide 2 keeps it.
This is where most creators lose attention.
THE TRAP
"Hi, I'm Dhruv..."
Nobody cares yet.
THE FIX
Hit the pain immediately.
Example:
"Most people don't fail because of lack of content.
They fail because they have no content system."
Now they want more.
THE CHECK
If Slide 2 became Slide 1...
Would people still keep reading?

RULE 03
Every Slide Must Work Alone
People save slides.
Share slides.
Screenshot slides.
A single slide should make sense without the carousel.
THE TRAP
"This is why..."
"The next reason..."
"The third step..."
Requires context.
Context kills sharing.
THE FIX
One idea.
One lesson.
One screenshot.
Every slide should survive alone.
THE CHECK
Send one slide to a friend.
Do they understand it instantly?

title: The DhruvWorld Content System: 15 Simple Systems for Consistent Content Creation
description: A comprehensive guide to building a content system that helps creators, founders, and students create content consistently without burning out.
body: ## Overview

The DhruvWorld Content System is a collection of 15 simple systems designed to help creators, founders, and students create content consistently without spending all day online. This system is built by Dhruv Solanki, a founder and AI creator, to provide a reliable and efficient way to produce content.
## Why It Matters

Most creators fail not because they're bad at content, but because they rely on motivation, which disappears. Systems, on the other hand, stay. The goal of this system is to create content without thinking about content every day, making it a game-changer for those who want to build a consistent content creation habit.
## Key Features
- Stop Creating Content From Scratch: Never start with a blank page. Capture first, create later.
- One Idea. Five Pieces Of Content: Turn one idea into multiple formats, such as posts, carousels, reels, and newsletters.
- Build A Content Vault: Save screenshots, notes, questions, AI discoveries, and founder lessons every day.
- Create For Search, Not Likes: Focus on creating content around topics that people search for, such as AI tools, founder lessons, and career growth.
- Use AI As A System: AI should amplify your thinking, not replace it. Use AI for research, structure, and drafting, and handle experience, story, and judgment yourself.
## Use Cases
By implementing the DhruvWorld Content System, users can achieve the following outcomes:
- Create content consistently without burning out
- Increase their online presence and visibility
- Build a content machine that produces multiple formats from a single idea
- Focus on search and trust rather than likes and motivation
## Example
Let's say you want to create content around AI tools. Using the DhruvWorld Content System, you can:
1. Research AI tools
2. Capture notes and questions
3. Create a post, carousel, reel, and newsletter around AI tools
4. Repurpose the content into multiple formats
5. Learn from the process and repeat
## Pros
- Simple and easy to implement
- Increases content creation efficiency
- Helps build a consistent content creation habit
- Focuses on search and trust rather than likes and motivation
## Cons
- Requires a mindset shift from relying on motivation to building systems
- May require some initial setup and planning
- May not be suitable for those who prefer a more traditional content creation approach
## Pricing
The DhruvWorld Content System is a free resource available on the DhruvWorld website.
## Best For

This system is best for creators, founders, and students who want to create content consistently without burning out. It's particularly suitable for those who want to build a content machine that produces multiple formats from a single idea.
## Final Verdict

The DhruvWorld Content System is a valuable resource for anyone looking to build a consistent content creation habit. By implementing these 15 simple systems, users can increase their online presence, visibility, and trust, while reducing their content creation time and effort. If you're looking to take your content creation to the next level, this system is definitely worth checking out.
prompt: Create a comprehensive guide to building a content system that helps creators, founders, and students create content consistently without burning out. Include 15 simple systems and provide examples of how to implement them.

title: 41 Master AI Prompts for Business
body: **DESCRIPTION:** A curated list of AI prompts for business, covering marketing, content, and more.
## Overview

41 Master AI Prompts for Business is a collection of AI prompts curated by Dhruv World, designed to help business owners and marketers create high-quality content, ads, and more using AI tools like ChatGPT, Gemini, and Claude.
## Why It Matters

These prompts can save businesses time and effort in creating content, while also improving the quality and consistency of their marketing materials. With the right prompts, businesses can leverage AI to automate repetitive tasks and focus on high-level strategy and creativity.
## Key Features
- 41 AI prompts covering marketing, content, and more
- Designed for use with AI tools like ChatGPT, Gemini, and Claude
- Saves time and effort in creating content and marketing materials
- Improves quality and consistency of marketing materials
## Use Cases
- Automate content creation for social media and blogs
- Generate ad copy and landing page content
- Create email campaigns and sequences
- Develop SEO-friendly blog posts
- Repurpose content across multiple platforms
## Example
For prompt #2, Monthly Content Calendar, the AI tool would generate a 30-day content calendar with one post per day, each with a content pillar, hook, brief description, and suggested format.
## Pros
- Saves time and effort in content creation
- Improves quality and consistency of marketing materials
- Allows for automation of repetitive tasks
- Can be used with multiple AI tools
## Cons
- May require some setup and configuration
- May not be suitable for all types of businesses or industries
- Can be overwhelming to choose the right prompts
## Pricing
Check the official pricing for the AI tools you're using.
## Best For
Business owners and marketers who want to leverage AI to automate content creation and improve the quality and consistency of their marketing materials.
## Final Verdict

41 Master AI Prompts for Business is a valuable resource for businesses looking to harness the power of AI in their marketing efforts. With the right prompts, businesses can save time, improve quality, and automate repetitive tasks.

**PROMPT:** ``` 41 Master AI Prompts for Business Curated by @_dhruvworld Note: These prompts can be used in ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, or any other AI tool you’re using. Marketing & Content 1. Brand Voice Guide You are a brand strategist with 15 years of experience building voice guidelines for consumer brands. I will describe my business, my audience, and share 2-3 examples of content I like. 2. Monthly Content Calendar You are a social media strategist who creates content plans for product and service brands. Create a 30-day content calendar for [platform] with one post per day. Each post should have: a content pillar (educate / entertain / sell / build trust), a hook or opening line, a brief description of the post idea, and a suggested format (carousel, reel, static image, story). Mix the content pillars so no pillar repeats more than 3 days in a row. Avoid generic filler content. Every post should have a reason to exist. 3. Content Repurposing You are a content strategist who specializes in making one piece of content work across multiple platforms without it feeling copy-pasted. Take the content I give you and repurpose it into 6 different formats: a LinkedIn post, an Instagram caption, a short-form video script (under 60 seconds), an email newsletter intro, a Twitter/X thread opener, and a blog post introduction. Each version should feel native to its platform in tone and structure. Do not just shorten the original. Rewrite it for each context. 4. Email Campaign Sequence You are a direct response copywriter who writes email sequences for small businesses and e-commerce brands. Write a 5-email welcome sequence for new subscribers. The sequence should follow this arc: email 1 is a warm welcome and brand story, email 2 delivers value with no selling, email 3 introduces the product or service with a soft pitch, email 4 handles the top 3 objections people have before buying, and email 5 is a clear call to action with urgency. Each email should be under 250 words and written in a conversational tone. No subject line clickbait. 5. Ad Copy Variations You are a performance copywriter who writes ad copy for Meta and Google campaigns. Write 5 variations of ad copy for the product or service I describe. Each variation should lead with a different angle: one leads with the problem, one leads with the outcome, one leads with social proof, one leads with curiosity, and one leads with a direct offer. For each variation write: a headline (under 8 words), primary text (under 125 characters), and a call to action. Flag which audience or funnel stage each variation is best suited for. 6. Instagram Carousel Script You are a social media content writer who creates educational carousel posts that generate saves and shares. Write a 10-slide carousel on the topic I give you. Slide 1 is the hook and must make someone stop scrolling. Slides 2-8 deliver the value in short, punchy points. Slide 9 is a summary or key takeaway. Slide 10 is the call to action. Each slide should have no more than 30 words. Use plain language. Avoid jargon. The goal is for someone to save this post and come back to it. 7. YouTube or Podcast Script You are a scriptwriter who writes long-form video and audio content for entrepreneurs and brand builders. Write a full script for a [YouTube video / podcast episode] on the topic I give you. Structure it as: a hook that opens a loop in the first 30 seconds, a credibility moment that establishes why the viewer should trust you on this topic, the main content broken into 3-5 clear sections, a real example or story inside each section, and a closing that tells the viewer exactly what to do next. Write in a spoken, natural tone. No bullet points. Write it the way a person actually talks. 8. SEO Blog Posts You are an SEO content writer who writes articles that rank and are actually worth reading. Write a [word count] blog post on the topic below. ```

title: Claude Content Automation Guide
description: Automate content creation with Claude using Skills and context files.
body: ## Overview

This guide explains how to turn Claude into a repeatable content engine using Skills and context files. It outlines a 7-step process to set up the system and create a workflow that produces on-brand content.
## Why It Matters

Content creators, marketers, and business owners can benefit from automating their content creation workflow. By using Claude, users can save time and effort in content creation, produce consistent and high-quality content, and maintain a consistent brand voice.
## Key Features
- Automate content creation with Claude
- Use Skills to teach Claude what to do
- Context files teach Claude how to do it in your voice
- Repeatable content engine with minimal hands-on time
- Troubleshooting and routine maintenance
## Use Cases
- Content creators who want to automate their workflow
- Marketers who need to produce large amounts of content
- Business owners who want to maintain a consistent brand voice
- Anyone looking to reduce their content creation time and effort
## Example

To automate your content creation, start by opening Claude and accessing the Customize page. Then, click Skills to open the personal skill workspace and browse the available skills. Download the ones that match your workflow and add them to Claude. Give Claude context with your brand files and run a single prompt to produce a batch of on-brand content.
## Pros
- Saves time and effort in content creation
- Produces consistent and high-quality content
- Easy to set up and maintain
- Can be used by anyone with a Claude account
## Cons
- Requires some technical knowledge to set up
- May require additional skills to be developed
- Can be overwhelming for those new to Claude
## Pricing
Check the official Claude website for pricing information.
## Best For
This guide is best for content creators, marketers, and business owners who want to automate their content creation workflow and produce high-quality content quickly and efficiently.
## Final Verdict

This guide provides a clear and step-by-step process for automating content creation with Claude. While it requires some technical knowledge to set up, the payoff is well worth the effort. With this guide, users can save time and effort in content creation and produce consistent and high-quality content.
